APOLLO'S FLAGSHIP RETAIL PRIVATE CREDIT FUND HIT BY REDEMPTION REQUESTS EQUAL TO 17% OF ASSETS; INVESTORS SEEK TO WITHDRAW NEARLY $15 BILLION FROM MAJOR PRIVATE CREDIT FUNDS

Redemption requests at Apollo's flagship retail private credit fund jumped to 17% of the fund's value during the second quarter, highlighting growing investor caution in the private credit market. Across nine major retail private credit funds tracked by the Financial Times, investors sought to withdraw nearly $15 billion, marking one of the largest waves of redemption requests the sector has faced.

author: First Squawk
title:
KOSPI PLUNGES NEAR 10% AS AI CHIP RALLY TURNS INTO A HARD SELLING SHOCK  

South Korea’s KOSPI was shown down 9.99% at 8,203.84 as chip-heavy selling hit Samsung, SK Hynix, and broader AI-linked names. The index had surged earlier on AI memory demand before rate fears and tech weakness forced a sharp reset.  The pressure is concentration. When one market leans too hard on semiconductors, one global tech selloff can hit the whole index.  SK Hynix and Samsung rebound only when AI memory orders stay firm. KRW, bonds, and Korean ETFs stay under pressure while foreign selling continues.

title: The biggest wealth transfer in history is here: how the next generation will spend the trillions
description: The greatest wealth transfer is underway, and heirs around the world who are set to inherit billions are preparing to use the money very differently from the generations that built it.
content: The largest transfer of wealth in modern history is underway, and the heirs set to inherit trillions of dollars in family fortunes are preparing to use the money very differently from the generations… [+4317 chars]

author: Christina Comben
title: Ethereum’s much-hated staking 'tax' may already be obsolete
description: Ethereum’s latest “funding crisis” has triggered a fierce debate: a contentious plan to tax staking rewards versus a new wave of labs and large ETH holders funding development offchain.
content:
Ethereum is running out of money, according to former insiders. 
The warning has sparked one of the fiercest Ethereum governance debates in months: should the network fund developers by taxing staki… [+8579 chars]
